systemRoles.name: Admin
systemRoles.description: Platform administrator with full access to all authenticated endpoints. The only role in the system — access is binary (authenticated admin or public visitor).

admin.notes: No granular permissions defined in specs. All 35 cookieAuth-protected endpoints use binary auth — if the user has the admin role and a valid session, they have full access. Every permissionMatrix in be_policy_flow.json has permissions: [].

superAdminUser.notes: First admin user created on initial deployment. Password must be changed on first login. This is the only user role in the system.

authenticated.description: Endpoints requiring cookieAuth session with admin role

public.description: Endpoints requiring no authentication — accessible by parents/visitors

notificationTemplates.name: Welcome Email
notificationTemplates.description: Sent when a new admin user is created

notificationTemplates.name: Password Reset Email
notificationTemplates.description: Sent when admin requests password reset via /api/auth/forgot-password

notificationTemplates.name: Trial Booking Confirmation
notificationTemplates.description: Sent to parent when trial is booked via /api/public/trials

notificationTemplates.name: Enrollment Confirmation
notificationTemplates.description: Sent to parent when enrollment is submitted via /api/public/enrollments

notificationTemplates.name: Trial Booking Link
notificationTemplates.description: Sent to lead when admin triggers /api/leads/{leadId}/send-trial-link

unknowns.description: No granular permissions exist in any OpenAPI spec or be_policy_flow — every permissionMatrix has permissions: []
unknowns.recommendation: Current binary auth model (admin vs public) is sufficient for a single-admin platform. If multi-role RBAC is needed later, add x-required-roles and x-required-permissions to OpenAPI endpoints.

unknowns.description: Admin role is inferred from frontend allowedRoles and be_policy_flow, not explicitly declared via x-required-roles in OpenAPI specs
unknowns.recommendation: Document this as a known gap. The be_policy_flow files consistently flag this (e.g., AD-GAP-01, LM-GAP-01, etc.) at low severity.

unknowns.description: Public endpoints for enrollment confirmation and payment instructions rely on unguessable CUID-based IDs for access control instead of authentication
unknowns.recommendation: Acceptable pattern for public-facing confirmation pages. Ensure CUIDs are cryptographically random.

metadata.notes: System uses binary authentication model: cookieAuth (admin) or no auth (public). No RBAC or granular permissions are defined in the current specifications.
